---
title: "Mesfin Genie"
pagetitle: "Mesfin Genie | Health Economist"
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
description: "Mesfin Genie is a Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at the University of Newcastle, specialising in health preferences, choice modelling and public health policy."
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero" aria-labelledby="hero-title"><div class="page-shell hero-grid">
<div class="portrait-frame reveal"><img src="/assets/mesfin-genie-profile-square.webp" alt="Portrait of Mesfin Genie" width="640" height="640" fetchpriority="high"></div>
<div class="hero-copy reveal"><div class="availability-pill"><span aria-hidden="true"></span> Open to collaboration</div><h1 id="hero-title">Mesfin Genie</h1><p class="hero-role">Senior Lecturer in Health Economics</p><p class="hero-affiliation">Newcastle Business School · The University of Newcastle</p><p class="hero-value">I study how patients and the public value healthcare, process information and respond to policy—using choice modelling, behavioural experiments and applied econometrics.</p><div class="hero-actions"><a class="button-primary" href="/publications/">Publications</a><a class="button-secondary" href="/cv/">Curriculum vitae</a></div><div class="social-row" aria-label="Academic and professional profiles"><a class="social-link" href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie" aria-label="University profile" title="University profile" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 10.5 12 5l9 5.5v1.7H3zM5 14h2v5H5zm4 0h2v5H9zm4 0h2v5h-2zm4 0h2v5h-2zM3 21h18v2H3z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&amp;hl=en" aria-label="Google Scholar" title="Google Scholar" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m12 3 10 5-10 5L2 8zm-6 8.5V17c2.8 2.7 9.2 2.7 12 0v-5.5L12 14z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1744-4666" aria-label="ORCID" title="ORCID" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8.1 7.7h1.5v1.5H8.1zm0 3h1.5v5.6H8.1zm3.1 0h2.3c2.1 0 3.4 1.2 3.4 2.8s-1.3 2.8-3.4 2.8h-2.3zm1.5 1.2v3.2h.8c1.2 0 1.9-.6 1.9-1.6s-.7-1.6-1.9-1.6z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://au.linkedin.com/in/mesfin-genie-ph-d-65634216" aria-label="LinkedIn" title="LinkedIn" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 4.5A1.8 1.8 0 1 1 5 8a1.8 1.8 0 0 1 0-3.5M3.5 9.5h3V20h-3zm5 0h2.9v1.4h.1c.8-1.1 2-1.8 3.7-1.8 3 0 4.3 1.9 4.3 5.2V20h-3v-5.1c0-1.7-.5-2.9-2.2-2.9-1.8 0-2.8 1.2-2.8 3.4V20h-3z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au" aria-label="Email" title="Email"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 5h18v14H3zm2 2v.4l7 5.1 7-5.1V7zm14 10V9.8l-7 5.1-7-5.1V17z"/></svg></a></div></div>
</div></section>
<section class="credibility-band" aria-label="Research profile"><div class="page-shell credibility-grid"><a class="credibility-item" href="/publications/"><strong>44</strong><span>research outputs listed</span></a><a class="credibility-item" href="https://www.grants.gov.au/Ga/Show/57849742-1674-4b10-b75a-47d575e5d430" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><strong>A$4.754m</strong><span>MRFF MandEval programme</span></a><a class="credibility-item" href="/cv/"><strong>6</strong><span>academic appointments</span></a><a class="credibility-item" href="/tools/"><strong>2</strong><span>live research tools</span></a></div></section>
<section class="section" aria-labelledby="research-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Research</p><h2 id="research-heading">Health decisions, preferences and policy</h2></div><a href="/research/">Research overview →</a></div><div class="pillar-grid"><article class="pillar-card reveal"><span>01</span><h3>Health preferences and choice modelling</h3><p>Patient and public preferences, discrete choice experiments, preference heterogeneity, equity and value assessment.</p></article><article class="pillar-card reveal"><span>02</span><h3>Decision processes</h3><p>Attention, information processing, numeracy, feedback, attribute ordering and eye-tracking evidence.</p></article><article class="pillar-card reveal"><span>03</span><h3>Public health policy</h3><p>Vaccination, mandates, telehealth, preventive care and economic evaluation of health programmes.</p></article></div></div></section>
<section class="section section-muted" aria-labelledby="recent-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Selected work</p><h2 id="recent-heading">Recent publications</h2></div><a href="/publications/">All publications →</a></div><div class="publication-teaser-grid"><article class="publication-teaser reveal"><div class="publication-kicker">npj Vaccines · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">An Open Repository of COVID-19 Vaccine Mandate Studies with a Worked Scoping Review of Quasi-experimental Evidence</a></h3><p>Fabio I. Martinenghi; Tushar P. Ojha; Anaïs Thomasson; Bianca Devsam; Sarah Drislane; Stavros Vasiliadis; Chiara Berardi; Aregawi Gebremariam; Arianna Dipalma; Teresa Gavaruzzi; Jeremy K. Ward; Francesco Paolucci; Margie Danchin; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth</p><div class="publication-foot"><span class="status-tag">Published</span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></div></article><article class="publication-teaser reveal"><div class="publication-kicker">Australian and New Zealand Journal of Public Health · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Community First or My Body First? Moral Values and Vaccine Mandate Preferences</a></h3><p><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Fabio I. Martinenghi; Francesco Paolucci; Jonathan Lake; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth; Margie Danchin; Teresa Gavaruzzi; Udo Evers</p><div class="publication-foot"><span class="status-tag">Published</span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></div></article><article class="publication-teaser reveal"><div class="publication-kicker">Health Policy · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Feeling Lonely? Preferences for Support Programmes to Reduce Loneliness among Older Adults in Australia: A Discrete Choice Experiment</a></h3><p><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Allison W. Boyes; Francesco Paolucci</p><div class="publication-foot"><span class="status-tag">Published</span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></div></article></div></div></section>
<section class="section" aria-labelledby="tools-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Tools</p><h2 id="tools-heading">Decision support and economic evaluation</h2></div><a href="/tools/">All tools →</a></div><div class="tool-page-grid"><article class="tool-page-card"><div class="tool-meta">Vaccine policy · Live</div><h3>eMANDEVAL Future</h3><p>Prospective vaccine-mandate policy analysis using predicted public support, expected lives saved and benefit–cost outcomes.</p><a href="/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch tool ↗</a></article><article class="tool-page-card"><div class="tool-meta">Farm economics · Live</div><h3>SOIL CRC Benefit-Cost Analysis Tool</h3><p>Farm-practice evaluation using net present value, benefit–cost ratio, return on investment and sensitivity analysis.</p><a href="/farming-bca-tool-v18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch tool ↗</a></article></div></div></section>
<section class="contact-strip"><div class="page-shell contact-grid"><div><p class="eyebrow">Contact</p><h2>Research, collaboration and supervision</h2><p>mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au</p></div><a class="button-primary" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au">Email Mesfin</a></div></section>
```
